subject: Alaska.Ai — LinkedIn Carousel No. 15 — 2026-07-23 — Governed First, Alaska Native Health and AI
html_body:
Alaska.Ai · LinkedIn Carousel No. 15

2026-07-23 · 9 slides · branch tsturg/adoring-shannon-8jclga

1 · Upload the document

Download carousel.pdf (2.8 MB, vector text) → LinkedIn → start a post → add a document → upload the PDF → title it: Governed First, Alaska Native Health and AI

2 · Paste the post copy

Data sovereignty is not a privacy setting.

It is a Tribe's right to govern what gets collected, who owns it, and what gets built on top.

A peer-reviewed paper documents Alaska's tribal health system doing both, adopting clinical machine learning and governing it first, under the CARE and FAIR principles.

Air evac in Alaska runs at least 40 times higher than anywhere else in the US.
Over 80% of communities sit off the road system.
A Cerner AI Scribe already runs in rural primary care visits.
A medevac decision-support model is still under development, not live.
In 2022, 35% of people surveyed had never heard the term AI/ML. By the 2025 symposium, far fewer could say that.

So who decides what gets built on that data, the Tribe that owns it or the vendor that ships the model?

#IndigenousDataSovereignty #AlaskaNativeHealth #ClinicalAI

3 · Post, then paste this as the FIRST comment (within a minute)

Sources, primary first
This whole picture rests on one peer-reviewed paper, dated May 23, 2026. Everything below traces back to it, so read it as documented governance context, not breaking news.
International Journal of Circumpolar Health, peer-reviewed symposium-perspectives paper, May 23, 2026, full text https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC13202658/
International Journal of Circumpolar Health, DOI record for the same paper https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/22423982.2026.2677426
Frontiers in Artificial Intelligence, companion community-engaged AI framework paper, April 7, 2025, same author team (not independent) https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12009764/
Every Alaska AI decision we track, with live deadlines, lives at alaskaaihq.com
Close to this work or see something we missed, add context in the comments.

Editor's note

Ship-worthy at 8.90 vs 8.3, zero hard fails, honesty handled with genuine craft (persistent provenance stamp, DEPLOYED vs PROPOSED discipline on S4, n=31 snapshot stamp, and S7's chart that refuses to invent a 2025 number). A deliberate starved-window governance-context deck built on one ~9-week-old peer-reviewed paper plus a same-team companion, framed explicitly as such on S8 and in the first comment, NOT breaking news; keep that framing when posting and route replies to the who-decides question. Paste the first-comment source block within about 60 seconds of posting.

Docket: closing soon

From the Alaska AI Docket, the public tracker this routine maintains daily.

Subscriber alerts sent this run: none due.

Automation changes this run (2)

The routine modified its own machinery this run. If a later week looks worse, this dated section is the rollback trail (each set reverts as one commit).
KindChangeRollback
fix
scripts		New scripts/dedupe_check.py plus a mandatory Phase 4 pre-flight wiring: given a candidate's --entities/--keywords (or --desc), it greps the FULL topic+angle+entities+keywords+headline text (never the truncated title) of every ledger/topics.json entry inside the 120-day window, drops a ubiquitous-AK+AI stopword noise floor (ai/alaska/data/2026/...), and scores each entry by distinctive named-entity phrase overlap + keyword-token overlap + distinctive-token Jaccard. It prints matches loudest-first and exits 1 on any LIKELY DUPLICATE (>=2 shared named entities OR Jaccard>=0.30), 0 on soft/none. Advisory signal that ADDS to the human dedupe gate; it does not replace or weaken it. Pure stdlib, offline, reads only.
why: Run 2026-07-23 primary reactive scar: the Phase 4 dedupe gate failed to catch that the intended lead (XPRIZE Wildfire autonomous-response finals near Nenana) was a near-exact repeat of Carousel No.5 (		revert the upgrade(2026-07-23) commit (deletes scripts/dedupe_check.py and the Phase 4 pre-flight prompt line; the dedupe gate reverts to unaided human semantic comparison)
pending upgrade(2026-07-23) commit (showrunner stages this set)
fix
scripts		gmail_draft.py now accepts the scorer agent's native score_report keys: 'passes' (alongside ship/ships) for the pass/fail banner, 'weighted_score' (alongside weighted_total) for the score, and derives the weakest criterion from the report card's lowest-scored criterion when no weakest_criterion/weakest_criteria key is present. Prevents a passing run from rendering the wrong 'Shipped below threshold. ? / 10' banner.
why: Run 2026-07-23 Phase 13: this run's score_report (weighted_score 8.90, passes true) rendered as 'Shipped below threshold. ? / 10 vs 8.3. Weakest: ?' because the script read only ship/we		revert the follow-up upgrade(2026-07-23) commit touching scripts/gmail_draft.py; the banner reverts to reading only ship/weighted_total/weakest_criterion keys.

Aftercare (this is where reach is won)

  • Post this draft Tuesday to Thursday, 8:00 to 11:00 AKT, for the strongest reach. Drafts arrive daily and you own the cadence, so hold it for a strong slot if today is off-peak.
  • Paste the first-comment source block within about 60 seconds of posting so the primary paper and companion links land under the deck immediately.
  • Reply to every comment in the first hour. Frame this as documented governance context on a single peer-reviewed paper, not breaking news, and route the debate to the who-decides question.
  • Check saves and comments after 48 hours. High saves signal the CARE plus FAIR reference card (S5) and the honest adoption chart (S7) landed.
